aliases: Churros with Dipping Sauce and Drink

english: Churros with Dipping Sauce and Drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has an entry for churro as a food product; this record represents a combo set including churros and a beverage. The FoodOn URI covers the churro component only. No composite combo-meal code exists in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Doughnuts and similar fried dough products) is the closest defensible category for the churros component. The beverage component is not separately coded here; the record represents the blended composite nutritional profile of the combo.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps specifically to churros or a churros-and-drink combo set as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard combo set comprising approximately 150–180 g of deep-fried churros with dipping sauce and a 120–150 ml sweetened beverage, totalling approximately 300 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Churros (SR Legacy #18617) and Sugar-sweetened Beverage composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for churros (USDA #18617) blended with a sugar-sweetened beverage component to reflect the composite 300 g combo set. The input record's per_100g values (energy 216.67 kcal, protein 2.67 g, fat 11.67 g, carbohydrate 25.0 g, sugar 15.0 g, sodium 116.67 mg, dietary fibre 1.0 g) are consistent with a weighted average of deep-fried churros and a sweetened drink and are retained as the working composite. Singapore HPB Foodo database was also consulted but does not carry a direct churros entry; USDA SR Legacy is used as the primary nutrient anchor.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for deep-fried wheat dough products
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for churros in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 74 is estimated by analogy to deep-fried wheat dough products (doughnuts GI ~76, fried dough GI ~72) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283) and the University of Sydney GI Database. The beverage component (sugar-sweetened drink) has a GI of approximately 63–68; the blended composite GI of 74 reflects the churros-dominant weighting in the 300 g combo. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (74 × 75.0) / 100 = 55.5, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 25.0 × 3 = 75.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (15 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100 g) in a combo that includes a sugar-sweetened beverage; AHA 2018 evidence links regular sugar-sweetened beverage consumption to elevated blood pressure independent of sodium intake.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this combo; if consumed, replace the sweetened beverage with water or unsweetened tea and limit churros to an occasional small portion, as the high sugar load and saturated fat (3.33 g/100 g) compound cardiovascular risk beyond the moderate sodium level of 116.67 mg/100 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~74) and high glycemic load per serving (~55.5), driven by refined wheat flour, 15 g/100 g sugar, and a sugar-sweetened beverage component, with only 1.0 g/100 g dietary fibre to blunt glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This combo is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the high-GI deep-fried dough combined with concentrated sugar from dipping sauce and a sweetened beverage creates a rapid and sustained postprandial glucose spike that is incompatible with glycaemic management goals.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying generates trans fats (0.17 g/100 g) and advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); saturated fat at 3.33 g/100 g and high sugar at 15 g/100 g collectively raise LDL cholesterol and triglyceride risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this combo; the combination of trans fat, saturated fat, high sugar, and deep-fried preparation represents a multi-pathway cardiovascular risk food that should be treated as an occasional indulgence at most, with no regular consumption recommended.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs that promote joint inflammation; high sugar (15 g/100 g) with low fibre (1.0 g/100 g) further drives pro-inflammatory mediator production.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this combo; deep-fried preparation and high sugar content provide no anti-inflammatory nutritional benefit and actively worsen systemic inflammation relevant to joint disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Churros and Drink Combo is a popular snack combo set sold through food delivery platforms in Singapore, featuring crispy deep-fried churros — elongated ridged dough sticks made from wheat flour, water, salt, and oil — typically served with a chocolate or caramel dipping sauce, paired with a sweetened beverage such as a soft drink, iced tea, or flavoured milk. The churros are piped through a star-shaped nozzle and deep-fried until golden and crunchy on the outside with a soft, slightly chewy interior, then dusted with cinnamon sugar. Originating from Spain and popularised globally through fast-food and dessert chains, churros are widely available in Singapore's mall food courts, dessert cafes, and delivery menus. Nutritionally, the combo is energy-dense at approximately 217 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate load (25 g/100 g), significant sugar content (15 g/100 g), moderate fat (11.67 g/100 g including 3.33 g saturated fat and 0.17 g trans fat), and low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100 g). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 74 (high), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 55.5 per 300 g serving — making this a high-impact food for blood glucose management. Clinically, this combo is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high GI, high sugar, trans fat, and deep-fried preparation. It is best treated as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular dietary item.
common_user_queries: Can I eat churros with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No direct churros entry exists in the Singapore HPB Foodo database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (churros, #18617) blended with a sugar-sweetened beverage component, consistent with the input record's composite per_100g values. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for deep-fried wheat dough products. Confidence is medium because the exact dipping sauce formulation and beverage type are unspecified, introducing variability in sugar, fat, and energy values.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite churros-and-drink combo is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite combo are not reported in the available approved sources for this preparation. 3. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per USDA FoodData Central for deep-fried wheat dough products with no fruit components. 4. GI of 74 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried wheat dough products (doughnuts, fried dough) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for churros exists in the approved GI database. 5. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was reviewed but does not carry a direct churros nutrient entry; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy is used as the primary nutrient anchor. 6. The combo is sold on GrabFood Singapore; the 300 g portion size is retained from the input record as a reasonable estimate for a churros-plus-drink set meal. 7. Dietary tag 'vegetarian' is retained from input; vegan status is uncertain due to possible egg or dairy in dipping sauce or dough formulation.

Iteration 1: Added glycemic_load_per_serving (55.5) calculated from GI × carbohydrate per serving / 100.

Iteration 1: Restructured health_context to include Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes (with correct human_condition_name), and Cardiovascular Disease with concern_level, key_factor, and clinical guidance.

Iteration 1: Populated aliases_by_language and flat aliases array with full multilingual coverage; verified consistency.
